description:
This map displays the spatial distribution of measurement points from two distinct datasets overlaid on a grayscale background imagery, which appears to be aerial or satellite imagery of a land area. An orange grid is superimposed across the entire image.
The legend indicates:
*   Blue triangles (▲) represent "measurement point from dataset 1".
*   Yellow circles (●) represent "measurement point from dataset 2".
The background imagery shows areas of bright, reflective structures resembling buildings or industrial complexes, particularly in the lower-left and central-right portions, contrasting with darker, less reflective areas that may be vegetated or open land.
Measurement points from both datasets are scattered across the map. Blue triangles (dataset 1) are concentrated in clusters, most notably over the central-lower built-up area, and a smaller cluster in the upper-left. Yellow circles (dataset 2) are more widely distributed, often appearing in proximity to or co-located with blue triangles, especially around the prominent built structures. Some yellow circles are also present in areas with fewer blue triangles, such as the top-right and along the bottom edge. No scale bar, compass, or specific geographic location labels are visible.
